ops.title: F1 live squash-engine no-op RESOLVED (root cause = role:system parse, NOT summarizer hang) + how to live-verify the proxy + the 429 secondary finding.
ops.body:
The F1 "live `ccs run` squash engine is a no-op" bug is FIXED on `main` (commits `fix(proxy): parse the in-messages system role‚Ä¶` + a `style(tests)` fmt follow-up; CI green). As-built recorded in `.claude/research/cc-squash-followup-plan.md` F1 section.

**Real root cause (observed):** Claude Code injects a `role:"system"` reminder (SessionStart-hook / deferred-tools notice, *string* content) INSIDE `messages[]`. The wire `Role` enum had only `user`/`assistant`, so `serde_json::from_slice` rejected EVERY real body with `unknown variant system`. That one parse failure silently disabled the whole engine: `stage_next`'s `parse_body` failed (no L1) and `body_model`'s `parse_body` failed ‚Üí model `"unknown"` ‚Üí L2 disabled. The prior plan's "off-path summarizer `decide()`/`fold()` hang" hypothesis was WRONG ‚Äî it was unreachable (parse failed first). Fix: add `Role::System` + a `segment.rs` arm (`SegmentKind::System`, `source_uuids=[i]`, `is_true_human=false`, no generation bump) ‚Äî a normal message-indexed squash candidate. The deepest enabling defect was *silence*: every `stage_next` early-return + the `init_econ` `"unknown"` fallback discarded their reason (now logged).

**Secondary finding (still true):** with parse fixed, `staged=0` persists live ONLY because the off-path summarizer hits `429 Too Many Requests` ‚Äî `stage_next` fires one `decide()` per candidate plus a `fold()` per turn, so a few back-to-back live sessions exhaust the account. Code fails open to Keep correctly (cardinal invariant holds). That staging produces a ref when the summarizer responds is proven by `stage_next_stages_body_containing_system_role_message` (mocked summarizer). Worth-considering hardening: throttle/serialize + back off on 429 for off-path summarizer calls.

**Why:** prevents a future session from re-chasing the stale "summarizer hang" hypothesis, and from mistaking the 429-driven `staged=0` for a code bug.

**How to live-verify the proxy (the mechanic ‚Äî not in the repo):**
- Build BOTH into one dir: `cargo build -p ccs-proxy --manifest-path crates/Cargo.toml` then `cp crates/target/debug/ccs-proxy DIR/`; and `cd go && go build -o DIR/ccs ./cmd/ccs`.
- Run `env RUST_LOG=ccs_proxy=debug,ccs_policy=debug,ccs_summarizer=debug DIR/ccs run --allowedTools Read -p "‚Ä¶"` (absolute DIR). `ccs run` cold-starts a detached `ccs daemon` via `os.Executable` (fusekit `proc.Spawn`), which spawns the SIBLING `ccs-proxy` (`ProxyBinaryPath`) ‚Äî so the absolute DIR makes the FRESH proxy run.
- `ccs run` does NOT rebuild ‚Äî an earlier "live proof" ran a stale `target/debug/ccs-proxy`. Always rebuild + copy first.
- `RUST_LOG` propagates through the self-spawn chain (shell‚Üí`ccs run`‚Üídaemon‚Üíproxy) but NOT under a launchd-managed daemon (its `Env` is only `{PATH}`). `warn!` ‚â• the default `info` filter, so warns show in `~/.cc-squash/daemon.log` regardless.
- Look for `L1 staged plan (shadow)` (engine engaged), and absence of `did not parse` / `model=unknown` warns. Proxy stdout+stderr ‚Üí `~/.cc-squash/daemon.log`.
- Restart race: after `ccs stop`, `sleep 1` before re-running or you hit `a peer owns the socket lock but is not answering yet`; if wedged, `rm ~/.cc-squash/daemon.{sock,sock.lock,port} status.json`.

Related: [[cc-squash-layer4-build]] (the L0/L1/L2 loop this fixes the live activation of), [[cc-squash-compaction-research]].
